q: When a friend is going through it, you:
options: Take over — I've already planned the recovery arc
Listen for hours without judgment
Show up with snacks and ride-or-die energy
Give them the hard truth they need to hear
Curated, aesthetic, gives nothing away
Warm, soft, cozy vibes
Reposting your bestie with 12 hype emojis
Hot takes and unfiltered opinions

q: How do you handle a talking stage?
options: I set the pace — they chase or they leave
I match their energy and stay patient
I go all in immediately, no games
I keep options open until someone proves themselves
Making them feel like the main character too
Knowing exactly what they need before they say it
Loyalty that would survive an apocalypse
Keeping them on their toes — they never get bored

q: Your ex would describe you as:
options: The one that got away
The one who loved too well
The one who would've done anything
The one who left them shook
Glow up so hard they regret everything
Cry, heal, journal, grow — the full cycle
Block, delete, protect my peace
Thrive visibly and let karma handle it
I plan the dates, the surprises, the aesthetic
I hold space, I listen, I stay calm in storms
I show up no matter what — 3am, across town, always
I push them to be better even when they resist
I need the spotlight a little too much
I give too much and lose myself
I'm too intense too fast
I test people to see if they'll stay

q: Pick a character you relate to:
options: The underestimated one who always wins
The quiet one with the biggest heart
The ride or die — loyal to a fault
The wildcard everyone's obsessed with
The one who organizes everything
The emotional support friend
The one who'd fight someone for any of them
The unpredictable one they love anyway
